title: Software Engineer, Early Career
description:
Software Engineer, Early Career
Engineering · Hybrid - San Francisco, CA or Seattle, WA
Superhuman offers a dynamic hybrid working model for this role. This flexible approach gives team members the best of both worlds: plenty of focus time alongside in-person collaboration that fosters trust, innovation, and a strong team culture. This role requires at least 2 days per week in our San Francisco or Seattle hub.
About Superhuman
Grammarly is now part of Superhuman, the AI productivity platform on a mission to unlock the superhuman potential in everyone. The Superhuman suite of apps and agents brings AI wherever people work, integrating with over 1 million applications and websites. The company’s products include Grammarly’s writing assistance, Coda’s collaborative workspaces, Mail’s inbox management, and Go, the proactive AI assistant that understands context and delivers help automatically. Founded in 2009, Superhuman empowers over 40 million people, 50,000 organizations, and 3,000 educational institutions worldwide to eliminate busywork and focus on what matters. Learn more at superhuman.com.
The Opportunity
We’re hiring early-career Software Engineers who want to learn fast, ship real product, and build AI-native software used by millions.
From day one, you’ll work on production systems- not toy problems- alongside experienced engineers who care deeply about craft, judgment, and impact. You’ll contribute to features across our core products, learn how large-scale systems are designed and shipped, and develop a strong engineering foundation in a modern, AI-first environment.
This is a role for engineers who are curious, thoughtful, and excited to turn ideas into real outcomes.
What You’ll Do
As a Software Engineer at Superhuman, you will:
Ship production-grade code that impacts over 40 million daily active users across our suite of AI-powered productivity tools
Build and iterate on features for Grammarly, Coda, Superhuman Mail, and Go
Work with cutting-edge AI technologies and modern web frameworks
Collaborate closely with product, design, and engineering teams to deliver delightful user experiences
Develop AI-native features that leverage large language models, natural language processing, and intelligent automation
Learn how to build large-scale distributed systems that are highly available and reliable
Learn best practices around system design, testing, deployment, and scalability
Contribute to technical discussions and help shape the direction of your team’s projects
Grow rapidly through mentorship, feedback, and hands-on ownership of real projects
Make a meaningful impact on the lives of millions of people who rely on our products every day
What We’re Looking For
Must-haves:
Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent practical experience), graduating in 2025 or 2026
Strong foundation in computer science fundamentals, including data structures, algorithms, and software design
Proficiency in at least one programming language (e.g., Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, Java, Go, C++)
Experience building software through internships, personal projects, open source contributions, hackathons, or coursework
AI native and experienced in using AI coding agents like Claude code, cursor, and Codex for productivity gains
Solid understanding of system design, databases, and back-end architecture
Excellent communication skills and ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams
A demonstrated ability to work independently with minimal guidance, proactively manage tasks and priorities, analyze and execute work efficiently, and thrive in a fast-paced, results-driven environment
Nice-to-haves:
Previous internship experience at a technology company
Experience with web tech (React, Node.js, TypeScript)
Exposure to AI/ML tools, APIs, or frameworks (e.g., OpenAI, LangChain, TensorFlow, PyTorch)
Familiarity with modern engineering workflows (Git, CI/CD, testing frameworks)
Track record of shipping projects end-to-end
Comfort navigating ambiguity and learning new technologies quickly
Compensation and Benefits
Superhuman offers all team members competitive pay along with a benefits package encompassing the following and more:
Compensation:
Base salary range: $130,000 - $165,000 (varies by location and experience)
Equity compensation
Benefits:
Excellent health care (including a wide range of medical, dental, vision, mental health, and fertility benefits)
Disability and life insurance options
401(k) matching
Paid parental leave
20 days of paid time off per year, 12 days of paid holidays per year, two floating holidays per year, and flexible sick time
Generous stipends (including those for caregiving, pet care, wellness, your home office, and more)
Annual professional development budget and opportunities
Mentorship program and structured career growth pathways
Superhuman takes a market-based approach to compensation so that base pay may vary by location. Our US locations are categorized into compensation zones based on proximity to our hub locations.
United States:
Zone 1 (San Francisco Bay Area, Seattle): $130,000 - $165,000
Why You’ll Love It Here
Learn by doing: You’ll work on meaningful problems with real users, not shadow projects
Strong mentorship: Learn from engineers who’ve built and scaled products at companies like Google, Facebook, and Dropbox
Real ownership: We trust early-career engineers with responsibility and support them as they grow
Connected culture: Highly collaborative teams, in-person connection, and shared wins
We encourage you to apply
At Superhuman, we value our differences, and we encourage all—especially those whose identities are traditionally underrepresented in tech organizations—to apply. Superhuman is an equal opportunity company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race or ethnic origin, religion or belief, color, gender expression or identity, sexual orientation, sexual identity, national origin, citizenship, age, marital status, veteran status, disability status, criminal prosecution, judgment in a criminal case, or any other characteristic protected by law.
For more details about the personal data Superhuman collects during the recruitment process, for what purposes, and how you can address your rights, please see the Superhuman Data Privacy Notice for Candidates.
